items.headline: City Council reviews $2026-27 capital spending plan
items.summary: The council held a study session on the Capital Improvement Program for fiscal year 2026-27, which outlines major infrastructure and development projects the city plans to fund. This is worth watching if you care about what physical improvements or changes are coming to Mountain View.

items.headline: Sunnyvale proposes changes to tenant protection rules
items.summary: The City Council introduced an ordinance to amend the Residential Tenant Protections Program, which could affect rental housing policies in Sunnyvale. Details on how these changes might impact tenants or landlords were not fully specified in the agenda.

items.headline: Library facilities and customer experience plan under review
items.summary: City council reviewed the status of the Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan on April 9, 2026. This plan addresses improvements to San José's library system and services.

items.headline: Downtown building gets bonus square footage for historic preservation
items.summary: The property at 405 Kipling Street is being elevated from Category 4 to Category 2 historic status, which allows an additional 5,000 square feet of floor area in exchange for seismic and historic rehabilitation work.

items.headline: Major mixed-use development proposed on San Antonio Road
items.summary: A rezoning request would allow an eight-story building with 167 residential units (28 affordable) and 1,400 sq ft of commercial space on San Antonio Road. This represents significant new housing and a land use change for the area.

items.headline: 333 Franklin Street zoning hearing postponed indefinitely
items.summary: A zoning hearing for a project at 333 Franklin Street has been cancelled with no new date set. Residents interested in this development should watch for a new hearing notice.

items.headline: City Council Authorizes Construction Contract with QLM, Inc.
items.summary: The Town Manager was authorized to execute a construction contract with QLM, Inc., though the specific project details are not fully described in the agenda. Residents should monitor follow-up reports to understand what infrastructure or facilities this construction work will affect.

items.headline: Grant awarded for Mexican Heritage Plaza School of Arts and Culture
items.summary: San José received a California Natural Resources Agency grant for the School of Arts and Culture at Mexican Heritage Plaza. This funding supports cultural programming and arts education in the community.
